item.title: There’s a Reason Taylor Swift Is America’s Most Carefully Studied Writer
item.description: By crafting vivid chronicles of her life since she was a teenager, she has built a vast catalog that rewards the attention millions have paid to it.

item.title: Young Thug Didn’t Just Mutate Hip-Hop. He Changed the Whole Act of Vocalizing.
item.description: He single-handedly changed hip-hop into something futuristic and alien — right when the genre needed it most.

item.title: Diane Warren Is a Titan of Lavish Power Ballads. You Adore at Least One.
item.description: Her widescreen, acrobatic songs have dominated radios, karaoke nights and — most likely — a big portion of your memory.

item.title: Lucinda Williams’s Songs Can Turn Any Sentiment Into a Lit Fuse
item.description: Few artists sculpt songs with this level of power and emotional intensity.

item.title: Stevie Wonder Is the Ultimate Embodiment of American Music
item.description: These are the songs you’d play to newborns, or alien visitors, to demonstrate the purest joys of our musical tradition.

item.title: The Objects That Inspire America's Greatest Songwriters
item.description: The objects and instruments (and occasional creatures) that spur the writing process.

item.title: Who Wrote Your Favorite Songs?
item.description: Test your knowledge of some of our songwriters’ biggest hits.

item.title: See Who Insiders Nominated as America’s Greatest Living Songwriters
item.description: We received more than 250 expert ballots. Explore some of them here.

item.title: How We Made the List of the 30 Greatest Living American Songwriters
item.description: First we polled hundreds of experts. Then Times critics spent weeks arguing about what constitutes greatness in American music.

item.title: Cast Your Vote for the Greatest Living American Songwriters
item.description: Times critics, with the help of more than 250 experts, made their list of the 30 greatest living American songwriters. Now it’s your turn to choose.

item.title: Do You Have to Make Pour-Over Coffee for Your Spouse?
item.description: A ruling on a dispute over a tedious cup of coffee.

item.title: The Rich and Powerful Want to Live Forever. What if They Could?
item.description: From the Kremlin to Silicon Valley, some of the most powerful people in the world now want something more: eternal life.

item.title: Bob Odenkirk Would Like to Remind You That Life Is a Meaningless Farce
item.description: The actor and comedian is keenly aware of humanity’s limitations, but he’s not giving up.

item.title: Longevity Science Is Overhyped. But This Research Really Could Change Humanity.
item.description: A new therapy has the potential to cure hundreds of diseases — and even reverse aging.
